vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1919: Consolidate per-issue resume/progress state into ONE git-tracked record (retire dual continues + fold in state.json harness/model)

plan.title: Consolidate per-issue resume/progress state into ONE git-tracked record
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Per-issue resume/progress state is scattered across five places; two ('continues') are a confusing duplicate, and the only non-derivable field (harness+model) lived only in machine-local state.json that never travels. An agent on a second machine cannot reliably know what has been done, and a local DB loss strands resume state. NOTE: the foundation already landed in commit e9914c9429 (record schema superset at src/lib/pan-dir/record.ts:97-120, sync-path status-override writer/reader, harness/model mirror, no-loss test — 63 related tests green). There is no existing spec or beads. This plan scopes ONLY the remaining gaps + verification; the work agent verifies-and-completes and does NOT rebuild the foundation.
narratives.Proposal: Close the remaining gaps so every write path lands in the single git-tracked record at <workspace>/.pan/records/<issue>.json (on the feature branch, git-tracked, travels via push): (1) fix the async dashboard split-brain that still writes workspace continue.json; (2) route the four remaining continue-state surfaces (done.ts, planning spawn, cloister readers, dashboard routes) to the record; (3) complete the one-time backfill to cover statusOverrides+harness+model at schemaVersion 2 (inspection-gated); (4) retire the legacy continues to read-only; (5) prove it with a no-loss/no-remaining-writers test and a cross-machine resume test. harness/model already resume from the record on a fresh machine (agents.ts:1096-1103) — this issue verifies that, it does not reimplement it.
narratives.NonGoals:
- CI guard and the enforced single-write-surface API are PAN-1921; this issue only stops legacy writes far enough to make the continues read-only.
- Reconstructing the cache from git + GitHub (boot replay replacement) is PAN-1920.
- Making pipeline verdicts reconstruction-safe is PAN-1922; keep the existing 'pipeline' mirror field unchanged.
- Keeping agent transcripts (JSONL) in git; beads cleanup; cache pruning/VACUUM (PAN-1847).
- Rebuilding the record schema or writer (already landed in commit e9914c9429).
- Mass-deleting the 132 historical .pan/continues/ files (kept as read-only legacy and backfill source).
- Adding a pan_records entry to projects.yaml (the record is branch-resident and needs no config).

autoDecisions.rationale: Unifies with the PAN-1908 records plane; retires the 'continues' name the vBRIEF maintainer disliked. Changing it now would churn the landed foundation.

autoDecisions.summary: Pipeline verdicts: keep the existing 'pipeline' mirror field unchanged; do not expand verdict handling.
autoDecisions.rationale: Open decision #2 leaned toward reading verdicts from the PR; PAN-1922 owns reconstruction-safe verdicts. Keeping this issue about work progress respects the epic's execution order.

autoDecisions.summary: Backfill targets only in-flight issues (live workspace/branch); 132 historical closed continues stay read-only legacy.
autoDecisions.rationale: Resume only matters for in-flight work; migrating closed issues with no workspace has no resume value and risks churn.

autoDecisions.summary: Continues become read-only legacy, not mass-deleted.
autoDecisions.rationale: AC6 explicitly allows 'removed OR read-only legacy with a migration'; preserving historical files avoids destroying the backfill source and audit history.

autoDecisions.summary: No pan_records entry added to projects.yaml.
autoDecisions.rationale: Design decision #4 makes the record branch-resident; its path resolves from the workspace. Backfill's leftover infra-repo coupling is reconciled to the branch path instead.

items.title: Fix async dashboard status-override split-brain (dag.ts → record)

narrative.Action: src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts:867-929 mirrorTaskOperationToContinueFile still writes workspace continue.json via readWorkspaceContinue/writeWorkspaceContinue (import at dag.ts:10), while the sync CLI path (vbrief/io.ts:370,402) already writes the record via writeStatusOverrideSync. Converge the async path onto the record using the async writeStatusOverride from src/lib/pan-dir/record.ts. Remove the continue.json read/write from this mirror path. No fallback chain — root cause.

items.title: When a task status change is applied through the async dashboard path, the new status persists to .pan/records/<issue>.json statusOverrides (not workspace continue.json)
After the async path runs, readWorkspacePlan returns the item with the updated status sourced from the record overlay
No writeWorkspaceContinue call remains on the task-operation mirror path in dag.ts (grep returns no production caller there)
Route done.ts continue read/write to the record

narrative.Action: src/cli/commands/done.ts:155,160,505,508 reads and writes workspace continue.json. Route those to the record: read decisions/hazards/resumePoint/sessionHistory via readIssueRecord, write sessionHistory/resumePoint via appendSessionEntry/writeIssueRecord (record.ts). Writes must queue a branch commit so they travel.

items.title: When done.ts loads resume context, it returns decisions/hazards/resumePoint/sessionHistory sourced from the record (readIssueRecord), not workspace continue.json
done.ts writes its sessionHistory/resumePoint updates to the record and the write queues a branch commit
A grep of done.ts returns no readWorkspaceContinue or writeWorkspaceContinue call
Route planning-spawn continue write to the record

narrative.Action: src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts:598 writes workspace continue.json to seed the planning sessionHistory entry. Route it to the record (appendSessionEntry/writeIssueRecord). H2: this changes the exact path that wrote this session's continue.json — verify a planning session still persists and resumes its sessionHistory via the record.

items.title: spawn-planning-session.ts persists the planning sessionHistory entry to the record instead of workspace continue.json
Starting a fresh planning session creates/updates .pan/records/<issue>.json with a reason:"planning" sessionHistory entry
A grep of spawn-planning-session.ts returns no writeWorkspaceContinue call
Route cloister context-builder readers to the record

narrative.Action: src/lib/cloister/work-agent-prompt.ts:142,362 and src/lib/cloister/handoff-context.ts:122 read workspace continue.json to build resume context (decisions/hazards/sessionHistory). Route both to read the record via readIssueRecord so cross-machine context is complete.

items.title: The work-agent-prompt resume context contains decisions/hazards loaded from the record, not workspace continue.json
The handoff context contains sessionHistory/decisions sourced from the record
A grep of work-agent-prompt.ts and handoff-context.ts returns no readWorkspaceContinue call
Route dashboard routes continue read/write to the record

narrative.Action: src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts:2884,2908 and src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts:1832 read/write workspace continue.json. Route them to the record via readIssueRecord/writeIssueRecord. Where an explicit workspace path is available, prefer getIssueRecordPathForWorkspace (H5). Server-reachable code must stay async (no execSync; use the async record APIs).

items.title: routes/agents.ts reads/writes continue state via the record (readIssueRecord/writeIssueRecord), not workspace continue.json
routes/workspaces.ts reads continue state from the record
No readWorkspaceContinue/writeWorkspaceContinue call remains in routes/agents.ts or routes/workspaces.ts
Complete the backfill to cover statusOverrides + harness/model at schemaVersion 2

narrative.Action: src/lib/pan-dir/records.ts:66-75 projectContinue copies only 6 continue fields (decisions/hazards/resumePoint/beadsMapping/sessionHistory/feedback) and writes schemaVersion:1; record.ts uses RECORD_SCHEMA_VERSION=2. Extend buildIssueRecord/records-backfill.ts to also copy statusOverrides (from workspace continue.json) and harness/model (from state.json), write schemaVersion:2, target the branch record path (.pan/records/<issue>.json), and run only for in-flight issues with a live workspace (D3). Decouple from the infra-repo-only gate (D7). This bead is inspection-gated: a wrong field map permanently loses state once continues are retired (H3).

items.title: buildIssueRecord copies statusOverrides from the workspace continue.json (when present) into the record
buildIssueRecord copies harness/model from state.json (when present) into the record
The backfill produces a record with schemaVersion:2 (matching RECORD_SCHEMA_VERSION), not 1
The backfill saves to the branch record path (.pan/records/<issue>.json) only for in-flight issues with a live workspace, and saves nothing for issues without a workspace
A backfill test on a sample issue passes, confirming decisions/hazards/resumePoint/beadsMapping/sessionHistory/feedback/statusOverrides/harness/model all persist into the record
Retire continues to read-only legacy (stop all production writes)

narrative.Action: Once all writers are routed to the record (beads above) and backfill is complete, make src/lib/pan-dir/continue.ts and continues.ts read-only legacy: remove or no-call their writer exports (writeWorkspaceContinue and project-continue writers), keeping reader exports for legacy/backfill. No production path writes workspace .pan/continue.json or project .pan/continues/<issue>.vbrief.json. Do NOT mass-delete the 132 historical continue files (D4/D5).

items.title: A grep returns no production caller of writeWorkspaceContinue, so workspace .pan/continue.json receives no production writes
A grep returns no production writer of project .pan/continues/<issue>.vbrief.json
continue.ts/continues.ts contains only reader exports for legacy/backfill, and a grep returns zero production callers of their writer exports
Status updates, resume, and planning still work end-to-end sourced from the record (typecheck + suite green)
No-loss / no-remaining-writers assertion test

narrative.Action: Add a one-shot assertion test (extending src/lib/pan-dir/__tests__/record-no-loss.test.ts) that (a) re-asserts the record schema carries all nine legacy fields, and (b) asserts no production source file (excluding tests, backfill, and legacy reader modules) references the retired continue writers. This is a one-shot test, NOT the enforced CI write-surface guard (that is PAN-1921).

items.title: The test passes, confirming the record schema contains decisions/hazards/resumePoint/beadsMapping/statusOverrides/sessionHistory/feedback/harness/model
The test fails if any production source file (excluding tests/backfill/legacy readers) contains a writeWorkspaceContinue or project-continue writer call
The test fails (red) if a new writer of continue.json is reintroduced
Cross-machine resume integration test (record-only resume)

narrative.Action: Add an integration test proving the headline acceptance criterion: a record written on a temp 'machine A' workspace (harness/model/decisions/hazards/statusOverrides/sessionHistory), then read on a fresh 'machine B' temp dir with NO SQLite agents row and NO state.json, resumes from the record alone. Exercises agents.ts:1096-1103 (record overlay wins) and readWorkspacePlan statusOverrides.

items.title: The test writes a record on a temp machine-A workspace then reads it on a fresh machine-B temp dir with no SQLite agents row and no state.json
On machine B, the resume read path returns harness/model sourced from the record
On machine B, readWorkspacePlan returns the statusOverrides sourced from the record
On machine B, the decisions/hazards/sessionHistory state survives the absence of DB and state.json, and the record alone returns it
Update legacy-continue tests for the record

narrative.Action: Update the tests that exercise the old continue plane so they pass against the record or assert read-only-legacy behavior: src/lib/pan-dir/__tests__/pan-dir.test.ts, src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/lifecycle-io.test.ts, src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/continue-state.test.ts, tests/vbrief/full-spec.test.ts. Do not weaken coverage — re-point assertions at the record.

items.title: The pan-dir.test.ts and lifecycle-io.test.ts suite passes against the record-based read/write or the read-only-legacy behavior of continue.ts
continue-state.test.ts and tests/vbrief/full-spec.test.ts compile and the suite passes after the writer migration
The full vitest suite passes with no new failures attributable to beads 1-7
